title: crotalaria auriculata
intro: Crotalaria auriculata, also known as the 'Eared rattlebox', is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, characterized by its distinctive ear-like shapes on the seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Its health benefits include the potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antacid. Crotalaria auriculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
